current_investigation.issue: test01 (legacy starvation test) failing with timeout (exit code 137)
investigation_timeline: User suspected journal logging changes broke test01
Temporarily disabled logger calls - test01 STILL failed (logging not the cause)
Discovered kworker denylist was added during THIS session (not original)
Removed kworker denylist to restore original test01_wrapper.sh
Added debug logging to sched_debug.c parser to trace which tasks are parsed
Rebuilt stalld and ran test01_wrapper.sh with debug output

key_discovery.finding: test01 blockee IS being detected, parsed, and boosted correctly by stalld
evidence: Parser debug showed: 'DEBUG: sched_debug parsed task: test01-51218 prio=120 ctxsw=3'
stalld detected starvation: 'test01-51218 starved on CPU 23 for 11 seconds'
stalld boosted blockee: 'boosted pid 51218 (test01) (cpu 23) using SCHED_DEADLINE'
test01 exited immediately after boost: 'restore_policy: failed ... No such process'
This proves stalld is working correctly and test01 completed successfully

root_cause.problem: Timing variability due to competing kworker starvation
details: Multiple kworkers are also starving on CPU 23 (kworker/23:1, kworker/23:1H, etc.)
stalld detects and boosts kworkers BEFORE test01's blockee
test01 blockee should be boosted at 5s threshold, but actually boosted at 11s
6-second delay is caused by kworkers consuming boost cycles
test01 completes successfully but total time exceeds 60s timeout
Exit code 137 = SIGKILL from timeout --kill-after (not test01 failure)

timeline_from_debug_log.initial_state: rt: 1 - starving: 4 (only kworkers, test01 not started yet)
timeline_from_debug_log.5s_mark: kworker/23:1 boosted (should have been test01 blockee)
timeline_from_debug_log.timeout: 60s timeout expires, test01 killed with SIGKILL

proposed_solution.rationale: Filtering kworkers eliminates timing variability, ensures test01 threads get priority
proposed_solution.current_status: ATTEMPTED but test01 threads not appearing when filter is active
next_steps: Investigate why test01 threads don't appear in stalld output when '-i kworker' is used
May need to check if ignore filter is too broad or has unintended side effects
Verify test01 threads are actually running and on the expected CPU
Consider alternative solutions if kworker filter doesn't work

stalld_parser_correct.issue_investigated: Thought parser had bug with leading spaces
stalld_parser_correct.user_correction: skipspaces() at line 406 moves ptr to first non-whitespace character
stalld_parser_correct.conclusion: Parser logic at line 429 is CORRECT - checks first non-whitespace char, not first char

observed_tids: 51217 = blocker after boost? (SCHED_FIFO prio 97, 19237 context switches - unclear)
test01_thread_structure.exit_behavior: When blockee gets CPU time, sets blocked=0, blocker exits, process terminates
